flags.description: The requirement prescribes the implementation technology 'SLAM' (Simultaneous Localization and Mapping) rather than stating the system need. At system level, the need is to autonomously generate a 2D indoor map; the algorithm used to achieve that is an implementation decision. Mandating SLAM forecloses alternative mapping approaches without safety or functional justification.
flags.recommendation: Rewrite to state the need: 'The system shall autonomously generate a 2D indoor map of the operational environment without prior environment knowledge.' If SLAM is genuinely constrained by system-level architecture decisions, document the rationale explicitly.
flags.reference: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 §6.3.1 (requirements should state what, not how)

flags.description: The localization accuracy of ±5 cm is stated without specifying: (a) the reference coordinate frame or ground-truth method for verification, (b) the operating conditions (speed, floor type, lighting), (c) the statistical confidence level (e.g., 95th percentile, RMS, or worst-case), and (d) whether this applies during initial mapping, re-localization, or both. Two engineers would implement and test this very differently.
flags.recommendation: Add conditions and statistical qualifier, e.g.: 'The system shall localize itself within ±5 cm (95th percentile) of its true position, measured against a calibrated ground-truth grid, during normal indoor navigation on flat hard-floor surfaces at speeds up to [X] m/s.'

flags.description: The term 'real-time' is used without a numeric bound. 'Real-time' is ambiguous in the absence of a defined maximum response latency. Additionally, the requirement does not distinguish between detection and avoidance response, nor does it define minimum obstacle size, detection range, or robot speed context.
flags.recommendation: Replace 'in real-time' with an explicit timing constraint, e.g.: 'The system shall detect and initiate an avoidance maneuver for static and dynamic obstacles within [X] ms of first detection, for obstacles with a minimum dimension of [Y] cm at speeds up to [Z] m/s.'

flags.description: The requirement states the system shall 'detect cliffs or stairs and prevent falling' but does not define: (a) the detection threshold (e.g., drop height >= X mm constitutes a cliff), (b) the stopping distance or reaction time after detection, (c) what 'prevent falling' means in operational terms (e.g., stop within Y cm of the edge). Without these, the requirement is not testable.
flags.recommendation: Specify detection threshold, reaction distance, and response: e.g., 'The system shall detect a vertical drop exceeding [X] mm and halt all drive motors within [Y] ms, stopping the robot no closer than [Z] cm from the edge.'

flags.description: 'Multi-room mapping and segmentation' is vague: the minimum number of rooms supported, the method of room boundary definition (automatic, manual, or both), and what 'segmentation' means in verifiable terms (e.g., rooms are identifiable as discrete named zones) are all undefined.
flags.recommendation: Specify minimum room count and segmentation criteria, e.g.: 'The system shall map and identify at least [N] discrete rooms, automatically segmenting them based on detected doorways or user-defined virtual boundaries.'

flags.description: The suction power threshold (>= 5000 Pa) is missing the measurement conditions: measurement point (at nozzle inlet? in free air?), floor gap/seal condition, and whether this is a minimum across all power modes or only the maximum mode. Without this, the requirement cannot be uniformly tested.
flags.recommendation: Add measurement conditions: e.g., 'The system shall generate a maximum suction power of >= 5000 Pa, measured at the main brush inlet with a standardized seal per [applicable test standard], in maximum suction mode.'

flags.description: 'Automatically adjust suction based on detected floor type' does not define: (a) which floor types are recognised, (b) what the adjustment is for each type (target suction level), or (c) the detection mechanism. Without these, there is no verifiable pass/fail criterion.
flags.recommendation: Enumerate floor types and corresponding suction settings, e.g.: 'The system shall automatically increase suction power to [X] Pa when carpet is detected and reduce suction power to [Y] Pa on hard floors, within [Z] seconds of floor type change detection.'

flags.description: SYS-FUNC-023 combines two distinct behaviors in one requirement: (1) detecting an empty water tank condition, and (2) notifying the user. These are separable functions that may have independent failure modes and test cases.
flags.recommendation: Split into two requirements: 'The system shall detect when the water tank is empty.' and 'The system shall notify the user when an empty water tank condition is detected, within [X] seconds.'
flags.reference: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 §6.3.2 (each requirement should express one idea)

flags.description: The requirement specifies automatic return to dock at battery < 20% but does not address: (a) what happens if battery reaches a critically low level (e.g., < 5%) before reaching the dock, (b) whether cleaning is paused or aborted, (c) dock-finding behaviour if the dock is obstructed.
flags.recommendation: Add failure/boundary conditions: e.g., specify behaviour if battery drops to a critical threshold before docking is achieved, and whether the cleaning task is saved for resumption.

flags.description: 'Resume cleaning after recharge' is underspecified: (a) no charge level threshold is given at which resumption occurs (e.g., resume at 80%?), (b) it is not stated whether the system resumes from the last position or restarts, (c) no timeout or maximum wait duration is defined.
flags.recommendation: Specify resumption conditions: e.g., 'The system shall resume cleaning from the last recorded position when battery charge reaches >= [X]%, continuing the uncleaned portion of the scheduled area.'

flags.description: SYS-FUNC-032 ('support automatic charging') is largely redundant with SYS-FUNC-030 ('automatically return to the charging dock when battery < 20%'), which already implies automatic charging. SYS-FUNC-032 adds no uniquely verifiable behaviour beyond what is covered.
flags.recommendation: Either remove SYS-FUNC-032 or rewrite it to cover a specific aspect not already addressed, such as the charging process itself (e.g., contact alignment, charge current management).

flags.description: 'Support cleaning scheduling' is insufficiently specified. It does not define: (a) scheduling granularity (daily, weekly, one-time), (b) number of concurrent schedules supported, (c) the interface through which schedules are set (app, onboard button), (d) behaviour if the robot is unavailable at scheduled time.
flags.recommendation: Add specifics: e.g., 'The system shall allow the user to schedule recurring cleaning sessions at a defined day and time, supporting up to [N] concurrent schedules, configurable via the companion mobile application.'

flags.description: 'Cleaning reports (area, duration, battery usage)' lacks: (a) report format or delivery mechanism (app notification, onboard display), (b) retention period for historical reports, (c) units and precision of each metric.
flags.recommendation: Specify delivery mechanism and data format: e.g., 'The system shall provide a cleaning summary report via the companion application within [X] minutes of session completion, including cleaned area in m², session duration in minutes, and battery consumed as a percentage.'

flags.description: 'Limit brush torque to prevent injury' provides no quantitative torque limit and no definition of the injury scenario (e.g., finger entrapment). This is a safety requirement that is entirely untestable as written. 'Injury' is also an undefined threshold in engineering terms.
flags.recommendation: Define a specific torque limit with technical justification (e.g., derived from a hazard analysis): e.g., 'The system shall limit main brush motor output torque to <= [X] Ncm under stall conditions, consistent with [applicable safety standard/HARA result].'

flags.description: 'Prevent battery overcharge and deep discharge' specifies no voltage/current/SoC thresholds for overcharge cut-off or deep discharge cut-off. Without numeric thresholds, the requirement cannot be verified and engineers have no design target. This is safety-critical as both conditions can cause thermal runaway.
flags.recommendation: Specify measurable thresholds: e.g., 'The system shall terminate charging when cell voltage exceeds [X] V per cell and shall inhibit discharge when cell voltage drops below [Y] V per cell, compliant with [battery chemistry standard].'

flags.description: 'Monitor motor temperature and prevent overheating' specifies no temperature threshold, no monitoring interval, and no defined response action (reduce power, stop motor, alert user?). As a safety requirement, all three are needed for verification and safe implementation.
flags.recommendation: Specify thresholds and responses: e.g., 'The system shall monitor motor temperature at intervals not exceeding [X] ms. If motor temperature exceeds [Y]°C, the system shall reduce motor power by [Z]%. If temperature exceeds [W]°C, the system shall shut down the motor and alert the user.'

flags.description: 'Cleaning coverage efficiency >= 95%' lacks: (a) definition of the test environment (room shape, area, furniture density), (b) definition of 'coverage' (sensor-confirmed passage, debris removal), (c) whether this applies per room, per session, or overall mission. Without these, test results will vary significantly.
flags.recommendation: Define test conditions and coverage metric: e.g., 'The system shall achieve a cleaning coverage of >= 95% of accessible floor area in a rectangular room of [X] m² with [Y] standard obstacles, measured as the percentage of floor area traversed by the cleaning path in a single session.'

flags.description: 'Maximum noise level <= 65 dB' does not specify: (a) measurement distance from the unit, (b) measurement standard (A-weighted dB(A)?, ISO standard?), (c) operating mode (maximum suction, minimum, mopping only). Results will differ substantially depending on these conditions.
flags.recommendation: Specify measurement conditions: e.g., 'The system shall not exceed 65 dB(A) sound pressure level, measured at 1 m distance in free-field conditions per IEC 60704-1, during maximum suction operation on a hard floor surface.'

flags.description: 'Operating time >= 120 minutes' does not specify the operating conditions: suction mode (min/max), floor type, mopping on/off, ambient temperature. All significantly affect battery life. Without these, the test is not reproducible.
flags.recommendation: Specify conditions: e.g., 'The system shall provide >= 120 minutes of continuous cleaning operation in standard suction mode on a hard floor surface at an ambient temperature of 25°C ± 5°C, from a fully charged battery.'

flags.description: 'Support secure OTA firmware updates' is vague: 'secure' is not defined in verifiable terms. It does not specify which security properties are required (integrity, authenticity, confidentiality, rollback protection) or reference a security standard. Two security engineers would implement very different controls.
flags.recommendation: Enumerate required security properties: e.g., 'OTA firmware update packages shall be authenticated using a cryptographic signature verified against the manufacturer's public key, and shall include a version counter to prevent rollback to a lower-versioned firmware.'

flags.description: SYS-SW-001 mandates a specific software architecture (three-layer: Application, Middleware, HAL) as a system-level requirement. This is an implementation architecture decision that belongs in the software design specification, not in the system requirements. It constrains the design without a documented system-level rationale.
flags.recommendation: If the layered architecture is genuinely required at system level (e.g., for safety isolation or supplier interface reasons), document the rationale and move to software architecture specification. Otherwise, remove from system requirements and state the functional need it serves (e.g., hardware portability, modular update capability).

flags.description: 'Support modular firmware updates' is vague. 'Modular' is undefined — it is unclear whether this means per-component updates (individual subsystem binaries), per-layer updates (HAL only), or delta updates. The term is not defined elsewhere in the specification.
flags.recommendation: Define 'modular' in context: e.g., 'The system shall support independent firmware updates for each functional subsystem (navigation, cleaning, connectivity) without requiring a full system firmware reflash.'

flags.description: 'Log diagnostic events with timestamps' does not specify: (a) which events qualify as diagnostic events, (b) timestamp resolution and reference clock, (c) minimum log retention period or storage capacity, (d) log access interface.
flags.recommendation: Specify event scope, timestamp resolution, and retention: e.g., 'The system shall log all fault events, state transitions, and sensor anomalies with a UTC timestamp resolution of >= 1 ms and retain the most recent [N] events in non-volatile storage.'

flags.description: SYS-HW-001 ('LIDAR or vision-based navigation sensor') uses 'or' to allow two significantly different sensor technologies. While this provides design flexibility, it should be ensured that SYS-FUNC-001 (map generation) and SYS-FUNC-002 (±5 cm localization) are verifiably achievable with either option. No specific defect here, but the alternative should be formally evaluated.
flags.recommendation: Confirm that both LIDAR and vision-based alternatives are validated against all navigation performance requirements before final design selection.

flags.description: SYS-SAFE-001 requires stopping 'all drive motors within 100 ms if lifted.' It is unclear whether 'all drive motors' includes only wheel drive motors or also the vacuum motor, brush motor, and water pump. The scope of 'all' is ambiguous in a system with multiple actuator types.
flags.recommendation: Clarify the scope: explicitly list which motors/actuators must stop within 100 ms. If only drive motors are intended, remove 'all' or replace with 'drive motors'. If all actuators are intended, state that explicitly and verify the 100 ms constraint applies to each.

flags.description: 'Prevent operation if the dustbin is removed' — it is unclear which operations are prevented. Does this include all cleaning and navigation, or only vacuuming? For example, should mopping and navigation still be possible without the dustbin?
flags.recommendation: Specify which operational modes are inhibited: e.g., 'The system shall inhibit vacuum motor operation and forward navigation when the dustbin is not installed, and shall alert the user.'

flags.description: SYS-FUNC-012 (detect carpet surfaces) is a prerequisite for both SYS-FUNC-011 (adjust suction) and SYS-FUNC-022 (lift mop). These three requirements share the same detection capability, but SYS-FUNC-012 does not specify the detection accuracy, false-positive rate, or response time. Poor carpet detection performance could impair the safety of mop lift (wet carpet damage) and cleaning effectiveness.
flags.recommendation: Consider adding a performance criterion to SYS-FUNC-012 such as detection accuracy >= [X]% and false-positive rate <= [Y]%.
